title: pinus halepensis subsp. brutia
intro: Pinus halepensis subsp. brutia, also known as the Calabrian pine, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to the Mediterranean region. The Calabrian pine has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antifungal, and antibacterial properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health issues. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting respiratory health. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, essential oils, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation, relieve stress, and boost the immune system.
